claims.claim: Apple's 5% Core Technology Commission (CTC) transition in the EU represents a strategic pivot from gating runtime execution to taxing raw transactional access, setting a precedent that will render traditional app marketplaces secondary to protocol-level commerce gateways.
claims.why_distinctive: It reframes Apple's compliance with the DMA not as a defeat, but as a proactive monetization shift from storefront curation (App Store) to device usage tax (CTC) which will apply to all alternate channels.
claims.falsifier: Regulatory bodies (like the European Commission) rule the 5% CTC illegal under the DMA, forcing Apple to charge 0% on external distribution without synthetic commission gates.
claims.verify: Examine whether alternative app marketplaces in the EU successfully maintain their businesses while paying Apple the 5% CTC on all transactions.

human_input.note: The director might emphasize that if the CTC survives regulatory scrutiny, Apple will successfully maintain its rent-extraction model even if Chrome or other third-party browser engines replace Safari's rendering stack.

claims.claim: The federal ban on Google's exclusive search default agreements will trigger a rapid fragmentation of user search traffic, driving users to browser-level and OS-level LLM local routers that dynamically select search providers based on intent rather than default placement.
claims.why_distinctive: It predicts that the loss of exclusive search defaults won't just benefit alternative traditional search engines (like Bing or DuckDuckGo), but will instead accelerate the shift toward local client-side intent routing engines.
claims.falsifier: Apple default search settings remain pointed to Google voluntarily without any financial transaction, maintaining Google's Safari search market share above 80%.
claims.verify: Monitor the market share of Google referrals on iOS devices 12 months after the implementation of the Judge Mehta remedies.

human_input.note: We should observe that Apple might build its own local web search index or LLM-based query router inside Siri/Apple Intelligence to capture search monetization now that Google cannot pay them for defaults.
